dimension: Completeness critic — whole categories missing from the draft + planning set
summary: Several production-grade whole-categories are absent or only named-as-data, not designed: backup/restore & disaster recovery (critical), the identity map's population/resolution mechanism, first-run onboarding + a Settings surface, GBrain cold-start bootstrap indexing, timezone handling for calendar coordination, application log/telemetry storage & retention, renderer error/degraded UX, and the Obsidian-Sync/out-of-band-writer interaction; a11y/i18n and a cost ledger are silently omitted rather than explicitly scoped.

findings.finding: No backup / restore / disaster-recovery strategy for operational truth. The operational DB holds event log, audit, approvals, outboxes, and connector cursors — these are operational TRUTH, not rebuildable (only read models are flagged rebuildable). Temporal's persistent SQLite (in-flight workflow history) is likewise local-only. If the Mac's disk dies, approvals/outboxes/audit/in-flight workflows are gone, with no recovery path defined. The per-workspace Markdown repos are repeatedly justified by 'Git backup' (DECISIONS.md:18) but NO git-remote/push policy, backup cadence, or restore procedure is specified anywhere. For a production-grade single-owner system that is the owner's entire system of work, this is a missing whole category.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 (operational store domains list — no backup); DATA_MODEL.md:13,26-34 (event log/audit/approvals/outboxes operational, only read models 'rebuildable'); DATA_MODEL.md:115 (Temporal/GBrain self-managed); DECISIONS.md:18 ('Git backup' rationale with no remote/push spec); PRD §17 Reliability/Portability (no backup/restore NFR)
findings.proposed_fix: Add an ARCHITECTURE.md §Backup & Recovery: (1) require a configured git remote + push policy (or documented local-only acceptance) for each workspace repo and the Global/Coordination repo; (2) define a backup mechanism + cadence for the operational DB, Temporal persistence, and Keychain export guidance; (3) define a restore/rebuild runbook (operational DB from backup, GBrain re-index from Markdown, read models rebuild) and an acceptance test analogous to the EVAL/clean-install tests.
findings.anchor: §4 Operational Storage / new §Backup & Recovery; §13 Deployment & Install

findings.finding: The cross-workspace identity map is named as stored data in six places but has no design: nothing specifies HOW the same person or company in two workspaces is matched, who curates the linkage, how merges/splits/disambiguation work, or how identity resolution respects WS-4 isolation (matching 'Maya' in Employer Work to 'Maya' in Personal could itself be a leakage vector). WS-3 only says the system 'shall maintain ... identity matching' with no mechanism, no contract, and no workflow.
findings.evidence: PRD WS-3 (§9.1, 'identity matching' — no mechanism); DOMAIN_MODEL.md:10,17 (Person workspace-scoped; 'GCL identity map stores sanitized cross-workspace links'); DATA_MODEL.md:10,34; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md:210 (GCL 'Stores identity map' — storage only, no resolution logic); THREAT_MODEL.md:14
findings.proposed_fix: Add an Identity Resolution subsection under §6 (Knowledge/GCL): define the identity-map contract (person/company canonical id, per-workspace aliases/external IDs, link provenance + visibility level), the population path (deterministic-first matching with human-approved links per WS-5, never auto-blended), and an explicit statement that identity linkage is itself gated by §6.3 levels so it cannot become a leakage channel.

findings.finding: No first-run onboarding flow and no Settings surface. The 9 enumerated renderer surfaces (§11) include no onboarding wizard and no Settings/Configuration screen, yet first run requires: creating the three workspaces, choosing repo paths, OAuth-connecting every connector, selecting providers and the per-workspace provider matrix, toggling the Employer-Work egress acknowledgment, and creating/locating GBrain brains. WS-6 presets and Phase-10 install hardening assume this exists but never specify the surface. The egress acknowledgment is said to live in 'workspace settings' (§16.5) but no Settings surface is listed.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §11 (surfaces list — no Onboarding, no Settings); PRD §8.1 (same 9 surfaces); PRD §16.5 ('shown in System Health and workspace settings' — settings surface never enumerated); PRD WS-6, §18.1 Phase 10 (presets/install hardening assume a setup flow)
findings.proposed_fix: Add Onboarding/Setup-Wizard and Settings to the §11 surface inventory with their data flows (connector OAuth, repo-path picker via preload/main, provider-matrix + egress-policy editing, workspace creation, GBrain brain init), and tie the egress-acknowledgment toggle and provider matrix to the Settings surface.

findings.finding: GBrain cold-start / initial-index bootstrapping is unspecified. The GBrain Round-Trip and parity spikes cover steady-state write-through and reconciliation, but nothing covers the FIRST index build over an existing (possibly large) Obsidian vault at install: indexing duration, progress UX, partial-failure/resume, and what the app shows while the brain is still warming. The still-open 'brain-per-workspace vs source' question (§19.1) and brain-per-workspace decision directly bear on how N brains get bootstrapped on first run.
findings.evidence: PRD §19 (GBrain Round Trip / Full Capability Bridge spikes — steady-state only); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 (GBrain rules — re-index/parity but no initial bootstrap); PRD §18.1 Phase 2 ('GBrain PGLite, GBrainAdapter' — no cold-start index step); §19.1 still-open brain-vs-source question
findings.proposed_fix: Add a GBrain bootstrap subsection to §6: initial full-index workflow per workspace brain (durable, resumable, progress-surfaced in System Health), behavior of dependent surfaces while indexing, and an acceptance bullet for first-run indexing of a seeded vault.

findings.finding: Timezone / calendar-time handling is an unaddressed whole category. LIFE-5 handles monotonic-clock correctness for SCHEDULE catch-up, but cross-calendar scheduling (CAL-1, P0) and brief 'intended-date' tagging have no timezone model: multiple calendars potentially in different zones, DST transitions, owner travel, and 'busy/free' comparison across workspaces all require explicit timezone normalization. Nothing in the PRD or artifacts names a timezone strategy.
findings.evidence: PRD LIFE-5 (clock-jump for schedules only); PRD CAL-1/§11.2/§6.4 (cross-calendar busy/free, P0 — no tz); PRD LIFE-2 ('tagged with the intended date' — no tz); grep of docs/planning: no timezone/DST/tz references anywhere
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Time & Timezone subsection (under §9 Workflows or §3 Domain): canonical UTC storage + per-calendar/zone normalization for availability and scheduling, DST-safe comparisons, and an explicit owner-home-timezone config; add a scheduling acceptance case crossing a DST boundary / differing calendar zones.

findings.finding: Application log/telemetry storage, rotation, and retention are undefined, and there is no explicit no-external-telemetry stance. Audit records and per-job AgentLogEntry are specified, but the operational LOGS of the control-plane worker and Electron main (crash logs, connector/provider debug logs) have no defined location, rotation, size cap, or retention. RET-1 covers raw capture but not logs. For a privacy-centric local app, the absence of an explicit 'no external telemetry/crash-reporting egress' decision is itself a gap.
findings.evidence: PRD §16.3 (audit only); PRD AgentResult.logs (§13.1, per-job); PRD RET-1 (raw capture retention, not logs); PRD §9.9/§17 Observability (System Health surfacing, not log persistence); grep: no log-rotation/telemetry references
findings.proposed_fix: Add a Logging & Telemetry subsection: log destinations + rotation/size caps + retention for worker and Electron main, redaction rules (no secrets/raw workspace content in logs), and an explicit decision that there is NO outbound telemetry/crash reporting (or, if any, that it is opt-in and egress-policy-gated).

findings.finding: Renderer error / empty / degraded-mode UX is unspecified. §11 lists surfaces and happy-path UX rules but never defines what the renderer shows when the worker/tRPC connection is lost, when the operational DB is in degraded mode (§4 mentions degraded mode only on the worker side), during long operations (GBrain indexing, connector outage), or empty/first-run states. For a production desktop app this is a required whole category.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §11 (surfaces + UX rules, no error/empty/loading/degraded states); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 ('app enters degraded mode, surfaces System Health' — worker-side only); §10 (event stream/tRPC — no reconnect UX)
findings.proposed_fix: Add an Error & Degraded-State UX subsection to §11: worker-disconnect/reconnect behavior, degraded-mode banner driven by System Health, loading/empty states for each surface, and how schema-rejection/outbox failures surface to the user (linking OBS-2 items).

findings.finding: Interaction of out-of-band vault writers (Obsidian Sync / iCloud / git pull) with KnowledgeWriter's compare-revision precondition is unaddressed. KnowledgeWriter assumes the only concurrent writers are itself and the human editing in Obsidian; but a single owner commonly runs Obsidian Sync or iCloud or a git remote on the same vault, which writes the working tree out-of-band and can change on-disk revisions between read and write. Multi-machine is deferred to V1.2, but a single user with Obsidian Sync enabled is a realistic V1 reality the design does not take a stance on.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §6 KnowledgeWriter rules ('compare-revision preconditions'); PRD §14.2 (compare-revision window assumes single fs writer); PRD §18.3 V1.2 ('Multi-machine sync hardening'); grep: no Obsidian-Sync / iCloud / git-pull conflict handling in any doc
findings.proposed_fix: Owner decision required: is Obsidian Sync/iCloud/git-remote on the vault a SUPPORTED V1 configuration? If yes, the architecture must define how out-of-band working-tree changes are detected/reconciled with compare-revision (e.g., fs-watch + re-read on conflict). If no, state it as an explicit V1 constraint/non-goal.

findings.finding: Accessibility (a11y) and internationalization (i18n) are silently absent — never mentioned in the PRD or any artifact. For a single technical owner they are reasonably descoped, but a production-grade architecture should state that explicitly rather than leave it as a silent omission, so /tasks-gen does not have to guess.
findings.evidence: grep of PRD + docs/planning: zero matches for accessibility/a11y/wcag/i18n/localization/screen reader
findings.proposed_fix: Add a one-line explicit V1 non-goal in ARCHITECTURE.md §Goals/Non-Goals: 'Accessibility-conformance (WCAG) and i18n/localization are out of scope for the single-owner V1; UI is English-only and not audited for a11y.' Keeps it a deliberate decision, not an accidental gap.

findings.finding: No cost/usage accounting (ledger) surface. Per-job cost is recorded in audit and a single 'agent cost' appears in System Health, but there is no historical usage/spend ledger (per-workspace, per-provider, over time). Aggregate spend caps are explicitly V1.1, so this is not blocking, but a minimal usage view is implied by COST data already being persisted.
findings.evidence: PRD COST-1/COST-2 (§9.11, per-job only); PRD §9.9 OBS-1 ('agent cost' in System Health); DATA_MODEL.md:32 (provider state 'cost/runtime defaults'); §18.3 (aggregate caps → V1.1)
findings.proposed_fix: Note in §11/System Health that per-job cost records persist and a basic per-workspace/per-provider usage rollup view is available (read model), even though enforcement of aggregate caps is V1.1.

findings.finding: Config-content versioning/migration and import/export are not addressed. Drizzle migrations cover DB schema, but evolution of config CONTENT (e.g., adding a capability to ProviderMatrix, changing EgressPolicy shape, new connector fields) and the ability to export/import workspace config for the 'open-source installable' goal are not specified.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md:108-116 (Migration rules cover DB schema only); DATA_MODEL.md:26 (Workspace config domain); PRD §5.2/WS-6 (open-source installability, presets) — no config export/import or config-version field
findings.proposed_fix: Add a config schemaVersion + forward-migration note for workspace/provider/egress config, and an export/import path to support presets and reinstall, under §3/§4.
